Title: Mid/Senior Data Scientist (Incogni)
HTML:
Our Data Science team, working with Surfshark and Incogni products, is looking for a skilled Data Scientist. Our projects cover a broad spectrum of data science challenges, from time series modeling to NLP and generative AI We follow a value-driven approach and are deeply committed to contributing to the growth and success of our products. If you are enthusiastic about cybersecurity, data protection, and data science, we look forward to your application!

If you want to:

  • Design, build, and productionize NLP-heavy machine learning systems, including text classification, entity extraction, semantic similarity, and LLM-based workflows;
  • Write clean, efficient, and testable Python code for data pipelines, feature engineering, modeling, and evaluation.
  • Own models end to end: problem framing, data exploration, model selection, validation, deployment, and iteration.
  • Work closely with product and engineering teams to turn ambiguous problems into concrete, data-driven solutions.
  • Evaluate and apply modern NLP approaches, from classical methods to transformer-based and generative models, with a pragmatic focus on impact.
  • Communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, focusing on decisions, trade-offs, and outcomes rather than theory.

And you can check off:

  • Proven experience in applied data science or machine learning, with a track record of owning and delivering models to production.
  • Strong NLP background, including hands-on experience with text modeling, embeddings, transformers, and LLM-based systems.
  • Advanced Python skills, with the ability to write production-quality code.
  • Solid SQL skills and experience working with large, imperfect, real-world datasets.
  • Strong understanding of machine learning fundamentals, model evaluation, and common failure modes.
  • Ability to translate business problems into modeling approaches and explain results clearly without relying on jargon.
  • Experience working in modern engineering environments, including code reviews, testing, version control, and CI/CD.

Here’s the deal:

  • Growth and learning opportunities: time dedicated to learning, conferences, online learning platforms, and books for your professional development;
  • Health and wellness: we want you to feel and be your best. That's why we offer various benefits, from online workouts and a physical coach to health insurance and regular mental health checks;
  • Tools of your choice: choose the technical equipment and tools you need to do your best work;
  • Community and celebrations: get ready for long-lasting traditions such as yearly workation, Friday get-togethers, various team buildings and company celebrations;
  • Work-life balance: We encourage working in the office, with the possibility of up to two remote days per week. Plus, with our WFA policy, you can work from almost anywhere in the world;
  • More days off: additional vacation days depending on your tenure;
  • Premium Surfshark accounts: for you, your family, and friends;
  • Gross salary: 3500-6800 Eur/month. Although it may vary depending on your skills and experience.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Are you ready to step into a vital role as a Data Scientist (junior or senior) and help the team build machine-learning based systems and scalable solutions? Then we have the perfect opportunity for you!




Banking Circle is a fully licensed, technology‑enabled bank processing over €1 trillion in payments every year - and growing. We exist to power the global economy’s most ambitious payments businesses with infrastructure that is fast, modern, and engineered for scale. Today, more than 800 people across 11 countries work together as one global team. We move fast, we think boldly, and we build with intent - because our clients depend on us to keep pace with the world’s most dynamic payments flows. We are expanding globally, scaling our capabilities, and deepening our impact, so if you want to shape the future of financial infrastructure - this is the place.




Your team welcomes you with open arms

As our new Data Scientist in the Data, Analytics & AI team in Copenhagen or London, you’ll experience an open and knowledge driven culture, where everyone can thrive, connect with colleagues, learn and grow. Here you will be empowered to make an impact every day and take ownership of the solutions you develop.




In this role you will play a key role in developing modern, machine learning–based systems at the core of the banks operations. Models to detect suspicious financial transactions, assess client risk, predict client behavior, assist and automate operations and tools to enable analysts to understand the model outputs.




We offer a hybrid work model for our employees to allow for flexibility and productivity.




Exciting work is waiting for you

Implementing cutting edge ML models at the limit of their scalability.
Take part in building scalable solutions as payment volumes are constantly growing
Identification of new use cases, actively seeking ways to use ML to improve the business and drive the design and implementation of solutions.
Take part in driving the analytics adoption across the organization.




Things we’ll look for on your resume

We are looking for a technically strong candidate with a solid academic background in engineering or mathematics, and hands-on experience in Python and machine learning. The ideal profile has at least 3 years of relevant experience and combines expertise in modern ML frameworks with solid software engineering practices, enabling end-to-end development and deployment of data-driven solutions. We value quality code, cake and coffee.




In this role you own the solution and have a strong interest in building scalable systems, leveraging data analytics, and applying machine learning to real-world challenges. We solve problems from start to finish, identifying and quantifying the business value, implementing the ML system including monitoring and defining operations playbook and interfaces to other parts of the organisation.




We work on a diverse set of ML problems. We are looking to reinforce the team in three specific areas. The ideal candidate would possess deep expertise in at least two of these domains:




Network / Graph Analysis

Experience with graph databases and tools (Neo4j, Memgraph, NetworkX)
Query languages such as Cypher
Graph embeddings and network-based machine learning
Operating and scaling large graph datasets

Geospatial Analytics

Address validation and geolocation
GIS data engineering
Working with OpenStreetMap / Google and related tooling
Spatial data processing and enrichment

NLP / Language Models

Classical NLP techniques (e.g. text classification, parsing, feature extraction)
Modern LLM-based approaches (embeddings, retrieval, prompting)
Building and evaluating production grade LLM solutions




Banking Circle is an international company. Our internal workforce consists of over 50 nationalities, and our clients and partners are located all over the globe. Therefore, our company language is English.

Title: Unsolicited: Business Intelligence & Data Science
Description:
Work With BI/data Science In An International Software Company
Are you motivated by making a difference and contributing to tomorrow’s BI/data science solutions? In which case you might be our new colleague.

You will create a strong foundation for strategic decisions
In your Business Intelligence or data science role, you will be working with various solutions that support Systematic’s business areas.

This involves the development of products and solutions for our customers and other business units – all in close collaboration with highly skilled colleagues.

Some of the tasks you will be working with involve:


Identifying and understanding the needs of both the customer and the product organisation through close dialogue
Using data to create value for our customers
Developing, coding and implementing BI/data science solutions
Cleaning and analysing data, and implementing and developing machine learning models
Facilitating workshops and building data models that support domains, user behaviour and software
Being part of a dynamic working environment characterised by collaboration, development and quality.



You can expect to work with a range of technologies, such as Microsoft Power BI, Python, Azure Cloud, Microsoft SQL Server, T-SQL, Microsoft SSIS and SSAS, data warehousing, data modelling and analysis.

High level of interdisciplinary expertise and collaboration
We are committed to acquiring knowledge and developing methods based on a well-functioning group of BI/data science colleagues.

We enjoy a very high level of job satisfaction, and the dynamics between experienced experts and young talents contribute to a high level of development and progress.

We work closely together across the different disciplines and areas of expertise, with teams comprising project managers, consultants, data scientists, software architects and business developers.

A key characteristic of the teams at Systematic is that we dare to challenge and develop ourselves and each other. We focus on knowledge-sharing in our work, and always find more innovative ways of doing things.

You understand the importance of BI and data science
You are creative, good at spotting opportunities and think out of the box. You have a solid understanding of the interplay between people, technologies and processes.

You have strong communication skills combined with sound business acumen, and you enjoy being involved in a wide variety of assignments.

You stay calm when faced with numerous tasks and requirements, and you maintain an overview and understand how to prioritise the tasks at every stage of the project.

You are organised, analytical and you are driven by the quality of your work. You enjoy presenting new ideas and have what it takes to garner the support of your colleagues and business partners.

At the same time, you are a team player who enjoys the prospect of actively contributing to the team and participating in development activities.

You are fluent in English, both orally and in writing. It is an advantage if you speak Danish, but this is not a requirement.

We work with security classified projects and therefore, as a Systematic employee, you need to have security clearance. Read more here:
https:
//systematic.com/en-gb/careers/security-clearance/ .

Any questions?

Please feel free to get in touch by emailing us at job@systematic.com. Unsolicited applications will continually be assessed according to current vacancies.
We store your details for up to six months, and if in that period your profile matches our requirements for a new job, you will be contacted to arrange an interview.



Connect with us!

Title: Senior Data Scientist - MTS (m/f/d)
Description:
We’re the world’s leading sports technology company, at the intersection between sports, media, and betting. More than 1,700 sports federations, media outlets, betting operators, and consumer platforms across 120 countries rely on our know-how and technology to boost their business.

Job Description

ABOUT US:

As a Data Scientist in Sportradar's AI unit, you will work withing an established, high performing team of experts on the design, development and deployment of predictive models and algorithms powering Spotradar’s products. You will leverage Sportradar's extensive sports datasets, betting transaction data and user interaction data collected through Sportradar's products and services reaching millions of end-users across the globe.

The position will be focused on the development of AI and advanced analytics solutions for sportsbook risk management and liquidity driven odds trading, collaborating closely with product owners and technical leads in our Managed Trading Services and Odds Services product domains.

Sportradar is a global leader in understanding and leveraging the power of sports data for hundreds of business customers around the world, in turn entertaining millions of sports enthusiasts.

THE CHALLENGE:


Analyse and explore data using statistical analysis tools and scripting.
Develop machine learning or statistical models for use in our sportsbook risk management and odds trading products and services.
Rigorously validate methods, models, algorithms and hypotheses using back-testing on historical data and/or simulations.
Bring models into production leveraging our AI platform, maintaining ownership of the AI solution throughout the model development lifecycle, from modelling to production deployment, monitoring and continuous model improvement.
Design and implement novel methods for data analysis, tailored to our sports-related application domains and propose new ways of using data to improve our products and services.
Present ideas and solutions to software developers and business stakeholders in a clear and understandable way.


ABOUT YOU:


The successful candidate will be knowledgeable in machine learning and statistics.
Strong programming skills and software development experience needed to productize low latency streaming analytics solutions are a must.
Experience with risk management and portfolio optimization in financial, gaming or sports betting contexts is a plus.
Experience in data science, statistical modelling, applied mathematics and/or software engineering.
Profficiency with advanced querying of large analytical databases using SQL.
Programming experience is a must.
Experience with Python, R or similar statistical and/or machine learning software/toolkits.
Bonus: Experience working in Java.
Bonus: Hands-on experience with real-time streaming analytics frameworks such as Apache Flink and streaming technologies such as Apache Kafka.
Bonus: Experience working with Mac/Linux environments.
Bachelor of Science in Mathematics / Statistics, Computer Science / Engineering, or related field; equivalent experience acceptable.
Fluent in English (written and spoken).
Autonomous, rigorous, creative and a team player.


OUR OFFER:


A collaborative environment with colleagues from all over the world (Engineering offices in Europe, Asia and US) including various social events and teambuilding.
Flexibility to manage your workday and tasks with autonomy.
A balance of structure and autonomy to tackle your daily tasks.
Vibrant and inclusive community, including Women in Tech and Pride groups which welcome all participants.
Global Employee Assistance Programme.
Calm and Reulay app (leading well-being apps designed to support focus, quality rest, mindfulness, and long-term mental resilience).
Online training videos.
Flexible working hours.


OUR RECRUITMENT PROCESS:


Initial Screening: A quick chat with our Talent Acquisition Partner to understand your background and expectations.
Technical Assessment: A short task to showcase your technical skills.
Technical Interview: Meet with the Technical team and Hiring Manager to dive into your solution, as also discuss team fit.
Onsite Interview: Meet with the local team and take a tour of our office for a final meet-and-greet.
Finals Steps: Receive feedback and, if successful, an offer!


Additional Information

At Sportradar, we celebrate our diverse group of hardworking employees. Sportradar is committed to ensuring equal access to its programs, facilities, and employment opportunities. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran. We encourage you to apply even if you only meet most of the requirements (but not 100% of the listed criteria) – we believe skills evolve over time. If you’re willing to learn and grow with us, we invite you to join our team!

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
I am working with a leading global quantitative investment firm that is looking to hire a Data Scientist to join its Commodities business in Aarhus.




This is a front-office facing role sitting at the intersection of Research, Trading, and Engineering, focused on transforming complex datasets into high-value research and trading inputs used across systematic commodities strategies.




The successful candidate will work closely with traders, quantitative researchers, and data engineers to onboard, transform, and productionise both traditional and alternative datasets that directly support investment decisions.




What you'll be working on

Partnering with Trading and Research teams to understand and deliver data requirements
Designing and implementing scalable data pipelines supporting systematic commodities strategies
Onboarding and integrating new datasets into the firm's research platform
Developing data transformation processes to create high-value, investment-ready datasets
Building tools and workflows to extract, clean, normalise, and aggregate data from diverse raw sources and formats
Managing the end-to-end lifecycle of new data acquisitions and integrations
Driving data quality, validation, and monitoring across critical research datasets
Troubleshooting complex data challenges and reducing time-to-production for new data sources
Researching and implementing innovative methods for data extraction and acquisition
Working closely with stakeholders across Trading, Research, Engineering, and external data vendors




This is a highly visible position offering direct exposure to front-office teams and the opportunity to work on some of the most interesting data challenges within quantitative investing.




What they're looking for

3+ years of experience in Data Science, Data Engineering, Quantitative Analytics, or a related data-focused role
Advanced Python programming skills, including strong experience with Pandas and NumPy
Experience designing and maintaining data pipelines and data transformation workflows
Experience working with financial, market, commodities, or alternative datasets
Postgraduate degree in Mathematics, Physics, Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, or another quantitative discipline
Demonstrable interest in financial markets and data-driven investment processes
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to operate in a fast-paced, high-performance environment
Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across technical and business teams
Prior experience within systematic trading, quantitative finance, hedge funds, asset management, commodity trading, market data, or alternative data environments would be advantageous




Please apply directly.




They are open to people looking to relocate, but you must have the expertise described above.

Title: Data Scientist (m/w/d)
Description:
Stellenbeschreibung

Du möchtest mit Daten Mehrwert schaffen und Unternehmen dabei unterstützen, das Potenzial von Künstlicher Intelligenz zu nutzen? Dann werde Teil unseres Teams und gestalte datengetriebene Lösungen für unsere Kunden – von der Analyse über Machine-Learning-Modelle bis hin zur Integration in operative Prozesse.

Was unser Team besonders macht? Ein starker Zusammenhalt, der über den Arbeitsalltag hinausgeht – ob gemeinsames Frühstück, Team-Events oder der spontane Austausch zu neuen Technologien. Wir arbeiten flexibel mit der Möglichkeit zum Homeoffice, schätzen aber gleichzeitig den persönlichen Kontakt im Büro, um gemeinsam Ideen zu entwickeln und voneinander zu lernen. Daher wünschen wir uns eine regelmäßige Präsenz von etwa 1–2 Tagen pro Woche im Office.

Aufgaben


Entwicklung und Training von Machine-Learning- und statistischen Modellen zur Lösung komplexer Fragestellungen
Sammlung, Aufbereitung und Analyse großer Datenmengen aus verschiedenen Quellen
Entwicklung von Predictive-Analytics- und Data-Mining-Modellen
Erstellung von Dashboards, Reports und Visualisierungen zur Ergebnispräsentation
Zusammenarbeit mit IT, Data Engineers und Business-Teams für datengetriebene Entscheidungen
Stetige Evaluation neuer Technologien und Algorithmen im Bereich Data Science


Qualifikation


Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium in Data Science, Informatik, Mathematik, Statistik oder vergleichbar; alternativ: mindestens 5-jährige Berufserfahrung im IT-Umfeld
Mindestens 3-jährige Praxiserfahrung auf dem Gebiet Künstliche Intelligenz oder vergleichbar
Fundierte Kenntnisse in Python oder R & relevanten Bibliotheken (Pandas, NumPy, TensorFlow, PyTorch)
Erfahrung mit SQL und NoSQL-Datenbanken
Sicherer Umgang mit Visualisierungstools (z. B. Tableau, Power BI, matplotlib, Plotly)
Verhandlungssichere Deutschkenntnisse (nachweisbar C1-Niveau) in Wort und Schrift sowie sehr gute Englischkenntnisse
Kenntnisse in Cloud-Umgebungen (AWS, Azure, GCP) sowie Verständnis für AI-Architekturen (u.a. RAG) von Vorteil


Was wir bieten


Bei uns findest du Kolleg:innen, mit denen die Zusammenarbeit Spaß macht. Wir begegnen uns offen, duzen uns über alle Positionen hinweg und denken nicht in Hierarchien oder Silos.
Du arbeitest meist direkt an deinem Heimatort – weil wir Kundennähe wörtlich nehmen und uns Work-Life-Balance am Herzen liegt.
Du profitierst von flexiblen Arbeitszeiten und hast je nach Kundensituation die Möglichkeit, von zuhause zu arbeiten.
Die richtigen Trainings und Zertifikate bringen deine Weiterbildung voran. Unsere E-Learning-Plattform Academia ermöglicht dir das Lernen, wo und wann du willst.
Einen Teil deines Bruttogehalts kannst du in CGI Aktien investieren – bis maximal 3 % des Monatsgehalts geben wir für jeden Euro einen weiteren hinzu.
Außerdem beteiligen wir dich am Unternehmenserfolg: Du erhältst eine Gewinnbeteiligung, die sich nach deiner individuellen Leistung richtet, sowie danach, wie wir als Unternehmen unsere finanziellen Ziele erreichen konnten.
Wir bieten verschiedene Modelle, damit du mobil sein kannst: Ob Bahncard, Dienstfahrrad oder Deutschlandticket.
Sabbatical oder Elternzeit werden unterstützt. Sie sind bei uns kein Karriere-Stopper!
Wir sind an deiner Seite, auch wenn es einmal nicht so gut läuft: Du kannst Sonderurlaub nehmen, und unsere Beratungshotline steht dir immer zur Verfügung.
Eine Vielzahl an gemeinsamen Events und Freizeitaktivitäten stärkt deine Verbundenheit mit deinen Kolleg:innen.


Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.

1976 gegründet und nach wie vor familiengeführt, ist CGI heute einer der weltweit größten unabhängigen Anbieter von IT und Business Consulting. Ein hohes Maß an Eigenverantwortung, Teamwork, Respekt und Zusammenhalt machen das Arbeiten bei uns besonders. Bei uns kannst du dein volles Potenzial entfalten!

Du darfst dich vom ersten Tag an als Miteigentümer:in von CGI verstehen. Wir lassen unsere Vision gemeinsam Wirklichkeit werden. Wir profitieren von unserem gemeinsamen Erfolg und haben die Möglichkeit und die Verantwortung, die Strategie und Ausrichtung unseres Unternehmens aktiv mitzugestalten.

Deine Arbeit schafft Mehrwert. Du findest innovative Lösungen und stärkst dein Netzwerk aus Kolleg:innen und Kunden. Gleichzeitig hast du Zugang zu globalen Ressourcen, um große Ideen zu verwirklichen, neue Chancen zu nutzen und von der immensen Branchen- und Technologie-Kompetenz zu profitieren.

Du bringst deine Karriere voran, da du in einem Unternehmen arbeitest, das auf Wachstum und Langlebigkeit ausgelegt ist. Du wirst von Führungskräften unterstützt, die deine Gesundheit und Zufriedenheit fördern – und dir Möglichkeiten bieten, deine Fähigkeiten zu vertiefen und deinen Horizont zu erweitern.

  • Entwicklung und Training von Machine-Learning- und statistischen Modellen zur Lösung komplexer Fragestellungen
  • Sammlung, Aufbereitung und Analyse großer Datenmengen aus verschiedenen Quellen
  • Entwicklung von Predictive-Analytics- und Data-Mining-Modellen
  • Erstellung von Dashboards, Reports und Visualisierungen zur Ergebnispräsentation
  • Zusammenarbeit mit IT, Data Engineers und Business-Teams für datengetriebene Entscheidungen
  • Stetige Evaluation neuer Technologien und Algorithmen im Bereich Data Science

Qualifikation

  • Erfolgreich abgeschlossenes Studium in Data Science, Informatik, Mathematik, Statistik oder vergleichbar; alternativ: mindestens 5-jährige Berufserfahrung im IT-Umfeld
  • Mindestens 3-jährige Praxiserfahrung auf dem Gebiet Künstliche Intelligenz oder vergleichbar
  • Fundierte Kenntnisse in Python oder R & relevanten Bibliotheken (Pandas, NumPy, TensorFlow, PyTorch)
  • Erfahrung mit SQL und NoSQL-Datenbanken
  • Sicherer Umgang mit Visualisierungstools (z. B. Tableau, Power BI, matplotlib, Plotly)
  • Verhandlungssichere Deutschkenntnisse (nachweisbar C1-Niveau) in Wort und Schrift sowie sehr gute Englischkenntnisse
  • Kenntnisse in Cloud-Umgebungen (AWS, Azure, GCP) sowie Verständnis für AI-Architekturen (u.a. RAG) von Vorteil

Was wir bieten

  • Bei uns findest du Kolleg:innen, mit denen die Zusammenarbeit Spaß macht. Wir begegnen uns offen, duzen uns über alle Positionen hinweg und denken nicht in Hierarchien oder Silos.
  • Du arbeitest meist direkt an deinem Heimatort – weil wir Kundennähe wörtlich nehmen und uns Work-Life-Balance am Herzen liegt.
  • Du profitierst von flexiblen Arbeitszeiten und hast je nach Kundensituation die Möglichkeit, von zuhause zu arbeiten.
  • Die richtigen Trainings und Zertifikate bringen deine Weiterbildung voran. Unsere E-Learning-Plattform Academia ermöglicht dir das Lernen, wo und wann du willst.
  • Einen Teil deines Bruttogehalts kannst du in CGI Aktien investieren – bis maximal 3 % des Monatsgehalts geben wir für jeden Euro einen weiteren hinzu.
  • Außerdem beteiligen wir dich am Unternehmenserfolg: Du erhältst eine Gewinnbeteiligung, die sich nach deiner individuellen Leistung richtet, sowie danach, wie wir als Unternehmen unsere finanziellen Ziele erreichen konnten.
  • Wir bieten verschiedene Modelle, damit du mobil sein kannst: Ob Bahncard, Dienstfahrrad oder Deutschlandticket.
  • Sabbatical oder Elternzeit werden unterstützt. Sie sind bei uns kein Karriere-Stopper!
  • Wir sind an deiner Seite, auch wenn es einmal nicht so gut läuft: Du kannst Sonderurlaub nehmen, und unsere Beratungshotline steht dir immer zur Verfügung.
  • Eine Vielzahl an gemeinsamen Events und Freizeitaktivitäten stärkt deine Verbundenheit mit deinen Kolleg:innen.

Together, as owners, let’s turn meaningful insights into action.

1976 gegründet und nach wie vor familiengeführt, ist CGI heute einer der weltweit größten unabhängigen Anbieter von IT und Business Consulting. Ein hohes Maß an Eigenverantwortung, Teamwork, Respekt und Zusammenhalt machen das Arbeiten bei uns besonders. Bei uns kannst du dein volles Potenzial entfalten!

Du darfst dich vom ersten Tag an als Miteigentümer:in von CGI verstehen. Wir lassen unsere Vision gemeinsam Wirklichkeit werden. Wir profitieren von unserem gemeinsamen Erfolg und haben die Möglichkeit und die Verantwortung, die Strategie und Ausrichtung unseres Unternehmens aktiv mitzugestalten.

Deine Arbeit schafft Mehrwert. Du findest innovative Lösungen und stärkst dein Netzwerk aus Kolleg:innen und Kunden. Gleichzeitig hast du Zugang zu globalen Ressourcen, um große Ideen zu verwirklichen, neue Chancen zu nutzen und von der immensen Branchen- und Technologie-Kompetenz zu profitieren.

Du bringst deine Karriere voran, da du in einem Unternehmen arbeitest, das auf Wachstum und Langlebigkeit ausgelegt ist. Du wirst von Führungskräften unterstützt, die deine Gesundheit und Zufriedenheit fördern – und dir Möglichkeiten bieten, deine Fähigkeiten zu vertiefen und deinen Horizont zu erweitern.

Title: Mid/Senior Data Scientist (Incogni)
HTML:
Our Data Science team, working with Surfshark and Incogni products, is looking for a skilled Data Scientist. Our projects cover a broad spectrum of data science challenges, from time series modeling to NLP and generative AI We follow a value-driven approach and are deeply committed to contributing to the growth and success of our products. If you are enthusiastic about cybersecurity, data protection, and data science, we look forward to your application!

If you want to:

  • Design, build, and productionize NLP-heavy machine learning systems, including text classification, entity extraction, semantic similarity, and LLM-based workflows;
  • Write clean, efficient, and testable Python code for data pipelines, feature engineering, modeling, and evaluation.
  • Own models end to end: problem framing, data exploration, model selection, validation, deployment, and iteration.
  • Work closely with product and engineering teams to turn ambiguous problems into concrete, data-driven solutions.
  • Evaluate and apply modern NLP approaches, from classical methods to transformer-based and generative models, with a pragmatic focus on impact.
  • Communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, focusing on decisions, trade-offs, and outcomes rather than theory.

And you can check off:

  • Proven experience in applied data science or machine learning, with a track record of owning and delivering models to production.
  • Strong NLP background, including hands-on experience with text modeling, embeddings, transformers, and LLM-based systems.
  • Advanced Python skills, with the ability to write production-quality code.
  • Solid SQL skills and experience working with large, imperfect, real-world datasets.
  • Strong understanding of machine learning fundamentals, model evaluation, and common failure modes.
  • Ability to translate business problems into modeling approaches and explain results clearly without relying on jargon.
  • Experience working in modern engineering environments, including code reviews, testing, version control, and CI/CD.

Here’s the deal:

  • Growth and learning opportunities: time dedicated to learning, conferences, online learning platforms, and books for your professional development;
  • Health and wellness: we want you to feel and be your best. That's why we offer various benefits, from online workouts and a physical coach to health insurance and regular mental health checks;
  • Tools of your choice: choose the technical equipment and tools you need to do your best work;
  • Community and celebrations: get ready for long-lasting traditions such as yearly workation, Friday get-togethers, various team buildings and company celebrations;
  • Work-life balance: We encourage working in the office, with the possibility of up to two remote days per week. Plus, with our WFA policy, you can work from almost anywhere in the world;
  • More days off: additional vacation days depending on your tenure;
  • Premium Surfshark accounts: for you, your family, and friends;
  • Gross salary: 3500-6800 Eur/month. Although it may vary depending on your skills and experience.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
About Us

What if your work could drive change in a globally established industry, shaping processes that touch every corner of the world? At Forto, we are at the forefront of change, harnessing the power of AI to revolutionise logistics. We want to reinvent digital supply chains to be transparent, frictionless and sustainable. From day one, our mission has been to simplify global trade – creating a seamless and efficient logistics process.

Your Role & Mission

As a Data Scientist at Forto, you will take ownership of production ML systems that extract structured intelligence from unstructured logistics data. You will working closely with the Engineering Manager across three core workstreams — document data extraction (FlashDoc), vocabulary mapping, and classical ML. Your immediate priority is ensuring continuity of existing production systems and setting up evaluation pipelines, but equally important is driving step-change improvements in accuracy through disruptive methods and new technologies when the opportunity arises. Beyond document automation, the team’s roadmap extends into traditional data science territory — demand forecasting, churn prediction, route optimization, and predictive analytics for logistics operations. You will bring both the ML engineering depth to maintain and innovate on current systems and the classical data science foundation to tackle these broader challenges as the team grows.

What You Will Do


Design, build, and maintain end-to-end ML pipelines for document extraction, classification, and data enrichment in production.
Build prompt evaluation frameworks and feedback-based optimization loops to systematically improve extraction accuracy.
Train custom in-house models using human-in-the-loop (HITL) data to move from assisted to fully automated extraction.
Build and maintain semantic similarity models for free-text to standardized TMS vocabulary across ports, terminals, container types, legal entities, and line items.
Improve pipeline reliability through redesign, testing, monitoring, and alerting for non-deterministic ML systems.
Evaluate and introduce disruptive approaches (new model architectures, fine-tuning strategies, novel evaluation methods) to achieve step-change accuracy improvements when incremental optimization plateaus.
Partner with Product Managers to identify where DS can solve real user pain points, proactively surface opportunities from the data, and shape product roadmaps with a data-informed perspective.
Collaborate closely with Engineering teams on integration, infrastructure, and API design to ensure DS outputs are consumed reliably by downstream systems.
Manage stakeholder expectations: communicate what is feasible given capacity, set realistic timelines, flag risks early, and negotiate prioritization trade-offs across teams.


Required Skills And Experience


2+ years of professional experience in data science or machine learning engineering
Ability to design, deploy, and maintain ML systems in production. Go beyond model development — it includes pipeline architecture, monitoring, reliability, and handling non-deterministic outputs at scale.
Ability to quickly get onboarded with new tools/ technologies/ problem space
Strong use of agentic tools for coding
Strong proficiency in Python
Hands-on experience with LLMs (prompting, fine-tuning, evaluation) and understanding of their limitations in production environments.
Strong foundation in classical data science and statistics: regression, classification, time series analysis, data leakage, experimental design, and hypothesis testing.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to work independently on ambiguous, research-oriented problems.
Demonstrated ability to evaluate when existing approaches are insufficient and propose disruptive alternatives — not just incremental tuning.
Strong stakeholder management skills: ability to identify problems and opportunities proactively, manage expectations on timelines and feasibility, and negotiate prioritization across competing demands.
Ability to commit fully to a direction after healthy debate, even when it wasn’t their preferred approach


Don’t fit all of our criteria? That’s okay! We know that you might be hesitant to apply if you don’t meet all our requirements, but here at Forto, we pride ourselves on embracing diverse perspectives and celebrating potential. If you are passionate about this position and the Forto values, please apply anyway. There could be a place for you in this role – or another one that’s a perfect fit!

Why work with us?

Our team is hard-working, constantly seeking to maximise the impact of their work, but we put our people first, always winning with care. We value efficient systems and swift, direct communication. We want everyone to have their time to speak, so that we can embrace diverse perspectives to help drive towards solutions always.

Title: Data Engineer/Data Scientist
Description:
Utveckla framtiden med oss!

Som Data Engineer/Data Scientist kommer du att spela en central roll i utvecklingen och integrationen av komplexa datalösningar för våra kunder. Du kommer att designa, utveckla och optimera data pipelines och ETL-processer, med fokus på skalbarhet, prestanda och säkerhet. Du kommer också att arbeta med realtidsdataprocessering och event-driven arkitektur, samt skapa och underhålla dashboards och rapporter i Power BI och Grafana. Ditt arbete kommer att innebära nära samarbete med tvärfunktionella team för att förstå affärsbehov och leverera datadrivna lösningar.

Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer innebära:


Designa, utveckla och underhålla data pipelines och ETL-processer.
Analysera stora datamängder för att identifiera trender och mönster.
Skapa och underhålla dashboards och rapporter i Power BI och Grafana.
Arbeta med Microsoft Fabric och Databricks för att optimera dataflöden och analysprocesser.
Samarbeta med tvärfunktionella team för att förstå affärsbehov och leverera datadrivna lösningar.
Implementera och övervaka datakvalitetskontroller för att säkerställa noggrannhet och tillförlitlighet i data.
Delta i utvecklingen av maskininlärningsmodeller och algoritmer för att förbättra prediktiv analys.


Du har dessa erfarenheter:


Kandidatexamen eller högre inom datavetenskap, statistik, informationsteknik eller relaterat område.
Minst 3 års erfarenhet som Data Engineer eller Data Scientist.
Erfarenhet av att arbeta med Power BI, Grafana, Microsoft Fabric och Databricks.
Stark kunskap i SQL och erfarenhet av att arbeta med databaser.
Erfarenhet av programmeringsspråk som Python eller R.
Förmåga att arbeta självständigt och i team, med stark problemlösningsförmåga.
God kommunikationsförmåga och förmåga att förklara tekniska koncept för icke-tekniska intressenter.
Flytande svenska i tal och skrift är ett krav
Minst en av följande certifieringar: DP-100, DP-600, DP-700 eller PL-300


Har du dessutom nedan är det meriterande:


Erfarenhet av molnplattformar som Azure eller AWS.
Kunskap om big data-teknologier som Hadoop eller Spark.
Erfarenhet av att arbeta med CI/CD-pipelines och DevSecOps-principer.
Erfarenhet av att jobba med hotbildsmodellering


Är du den vi söker?

Om oss

Omegapoint grundades 2001 och är Norra Europas ledande konsultbolag inom cybersäker digitalisering. Med en vision om en framtid där vi fullt ut kan lita på teknik använder vi vår expertis för att säkra våra kunders och medarbetares utveckling. Vi är vassa och snälla, något som karaktäriseras av vår kompetensutvecklingskultur och viljan att dela vår kunskap med varandra. För närvarande är vi över 900 anställda konsulter i Stockholm, Oslo, Drammen, Köpenhamn, Göteborg, Malmö, Örebro, Västerås, Enköping, Uppsala, Umeå och Montréal.

Vi satsar på dig

Vi vågar lova dig en arbetsplats där du ges mängder med möjligheter att fortsätta utvecklas.

På Omegapoint sätter vi din kompetens i centrum och ger dig alla förutsättningar för ett utvecklande och varierat jobb. Som konsult hos oss tillbringar du vardagen i uppdrag hos någon av våra kunder, men har alltid en välkomnande hemmahamn på kontoret i centrala Örebro eller Västerås. Här ordnar vi mängder av aktiviteter under året för att vi ska utvecklas och ligga i framkant. Det är en del i vår kompetenskultur som bygger på samarbete och en vilja att stärka varandra.

Med en stor och varierande kundbas, från små specialistföretag till stora och välkända företag och myndigheter, har vi en möjlighet att erbjuda utmanande och roliga uppdrag.

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

Har några frågor kring tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta agneta.lindqvist@omegapoint.se

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
What you’ll do


Train, test, and ship models that power Peec AI’s recommendations - helping customers boost their visibility in AI search
Develop algorithms that extract actionable insights from AI search behavior, creating data-driven recommendations that help brands increase their AI Search visibility
Own the full model lifecycle from experimentation to production deployment, working closely with engineering to integrate ML solutions into our systems
Design and implement data pipelines to ingest, process, and analyze large volumes of data


What we’re looking for


Proven backend development skills in Python with experience building APIs, data pipelines, or ML infrastructure, and familiarity with tools like FastAPI, Docker, and cloud platforms (preferably GCP)
Deep curiosity about how LLMs work, with the ability to reverse-engineer AI search behavior and translate patterns into actionable product features
Track record of taking projects from research to production in a fast-moving startup environment, with strong problem-solving skills and comfort working with ambiguous, evolving problems
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to customers and stakeholders


Our Data Science Stack


Languages: Python, SQL
Libraries: Pandas, NumPy, HuggingFace, PyTorch, TensorFlow, ONNX
Backend: GCP, Cloud Functions, Firestore, Postgres, AlloyDB, BigQuery
AI Models: OpenAI, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini, Llama, etc.


Bonus Points


Contributions to open-source projects
Deployed side/hobby projects that we can check out
Presented research papers at top ML or AI conferences
Having started a company before or worked at a high-growth startup
Fluency in Typescript


What we offer


Exciting and challenging work with real impact and ownership at one of Europe’s fastest-growing Series A startups
Regular team events and off-sites
Aggressive equity compensation package
Paid Dinner & Uber home when working late
The most beautiful office space and work environment in Berlin

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist




We bouwen met Just Good People een nextgen data team met leuke mensen met sterke data, AI, human & biz skills. Geen PPT-consultancy, maar datakansen kraken en echt waarde realiseren bij toffe klanten. En veel opleiding.




Zie jij in bergen info – naast getallen – vooral kansen? Wil je die liever omzetten in actie dan in rapporten die in een la verdwijnen?




Wil je maximaal leren door in korte tijd bij meerdere bedrijven in de keuken te kijken? Terwijl wij je helpen ontwikkelen op het snijvlak van data, business en people?




Dan hebben we de uitdaging voor jou.




🟡 Just Good People

Bij JGP hebben we twee simpele regels: We nemen goede mensen aan. En we zorgen goed voor deze mensen. Dat betekent dat we samen optrekken om jou maximaal in je kracht te krijgen op een manier die bij jou past.







🌱 Unieke data filosofie

“As machines get better at being machines, humans have to get better at being more human.”

Deze nieuwe tijd vraagt om een nieuw soort data professional. Bij Just Good People Data helpen we bedrijven versnellen met top notch data professionals met goed ontwikkelde data, business én human skills. En trainen je naar nog hoger niveau. Met aandacht voor:




Volgende levels in Data | AI
“Waarde denken” voor klanten/ gebruikers
Stakeholder management
Communiceren & inspireren







⚡ Wat je gaat doen

Je werkt aan projecten bij mooie bedrijven zoals Bol.com, Defensie, Action, Rabo, NS, AMC, ASML, Essent.




Denk aan:

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Beneath a simple yet elegant user interface lies a complex system of functionalities. Our AI-powered product uses advanced image recognition, document detection, data extraction, fraud detection, and behavior analysis to make identity decisions. We help our partners build onboarding processes that ensure good people get things done and bad folks get stopped. In order to take our product to the next level, we need world-class data scientists to automate processes historically reserved for humans, and do so at superhuman performance levels. We are making significant investments to create a top-notch machine learning platform for prototyping, training, deploying, and monitoring machine learning models at a massive scale. Together with our world-class data annotation, data curation and MLOps teams, we fuel the AI-powered engine behind our success.

As a Senior Data Scientist for the Physical Identities team, your primary responsibility will be to design, build, deploy and monitor machine learning models and solutions specifically tailored for our identity verification products. In this role, you will collaborate closely with Product Managers, Data Scientists, Machine Learning Engineers, Software Engineers and other data professionals to develop state-of-the-art document detection and classification, data extraction and fraud detection algorithms. You will drive projects like, document detection and classification, OCR extraction and validation, supervised/unsupervised detection of anomalies in physical documents or document fraud risk scoring systems and leverage our large knowledge base and production data volumes to take these initiatives from initial prototype through full production deployment.

You’ll Help Us Protect Honest People Online By


Owning end-to-end responsibility for building identity verification machine learning models from prototyping to production and and driving measurable impact in live environments
Taking full ownership for performance metrics like verification accuracy and false acceptance rates, and pushing the boundaries of what’s possible under high-security constraints
Influencing and shaping our in-house models, and evaluating external solution vendors, and improving instrumentation to generate valuable insights while creating new machine learning products
Establishing key metrics, deriving data-driven insights, and conducting experiments to assess the overall product impact of our biometric machine learning solutions on identity verification
Working both independently and collaboratively with other engineers to define and deliver high-impact product features in the biometrics domain



You are the right future Veriffian for the job if you have:


Previous end-to-end professional experience in the identity verification industry, successfully delivering high-performance machine learning models in the verification of physical identity documents.
Theoretical and applied knowledge in Machine Learning concepts with a special focus on Computer Vision, Data Extraction and Anomaly Detection techniques
Hands-on experience working with Deep Learning toolkits such as Tensorflow or PyTorch and Gradient Boosted models libraries like XGBoost or Catboost.
Great product thinking skills.
Proficiency in SQL
Can effectively communicate the results and trade-offs to both technical and non-technical audiences
Enjoy working in a fast-paced, highly fluid, and multi-functional environment.



Why Veriff?

We are the preferred identity verification platform partner for the world’s most innovative growth-driven organizations helping conveniently verify and safeguard users anywhere in the world. We support the broadest number of identity documents from nearly every country and territory in the world - and this is continually increasing! With a diverse team in the United States, United Kingdom, Spain, and Estonia, as well as robust backing and funding from investors including Accel, Alkeon, IVP, Tiger Capital, and Y Combinator, we’re dedicated to helping businesses and individuals build a safer and more secure world. We strive to be the benchmark for trust online, and we take pride in being a positive force.

When you join Team Veriff, we offer you the opportunity to have a real impact and advance your career, while looking after you along the way with a range of benefits designed with you in mind. Some of our favorites include...


Flexibility to work from home
Stock options that ensure your share in our success
Extra recharge days on top of your annual vacation
Comprehensive relocation support to Estonia or Spain
Extensive medical, dental, and vision insurance to ensure you’re feeling great physically and mentally
Learning and Development & Health and Sports budget that you are free to tailor to your own needs
Four weeks of fully paid sabbatical leave after reaching your 5th work anniversary



We are an Equal Opportunities employer committed to a diverse and representative team. Different opinions, perspectives and personalities push us forward. We want to hear from people who are passionate about their work and align with our values. Regardless of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status or disability. We also know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates - so rest assured, whoever you are, we want to hear from you!

Please be informed that in the final stage of the recruitment process we may request you to go through identity verification. For more information, please see Recruitment Privacy Policy. #SPBTAL

Title: Full Stack Data Scientist
Description:
Are you a Full Stack Data Scientist driven by turning data into tangible business results? Do you want to help organizations become more productive and competitive?

Vuono Group is now looking for Full Stack Data Scientists to join our growing team of experts in Helsinki. Vuono Group is a consultancy that drives business value with processes and AI. We empower Nordic organisations in both the private and public sectors to bridge the gap between business needs and AI capabilities, enabling smarter decision-making, increased operational efficiency, and accelerated growth.

About The Role

As a Full Stack Data Scientist, you are interested in what happens under the hood of modern data solutions and data architectures. You care about data quality, usability and the value it can provide for various business processes. You're experienced with data science and engineering practices, including data observability, implementing MLOps (maybe even LLMOps) workflows and real-time data processing. You understand the intersection of ML/AI systems and data engineering and can build scalable pipelines that support both analytical and operational use cases. Experience with cloud-native data architectures (e.g. Azure, AWS, GCP) is essential.

At Vuono Group, you will get to work in a cross-competence team making our clients' businesses more intelligent with data. In practice, this means building data pipelines, integrating systems together, and utilising ML and AI for insights and operational use cases.

What We’re Looking For


Experience in planning and building production level data solutions that solve real business problems
An analytical mindset and problem-solving skills
Machine learning modeling and/or natural language processing experience
Experience in designing and implementing end-to-end data pipelines that transform raw operational data into business-ready information products
Experience in running and monitoring production workloads
Programming experience in Python and SQL
Willingness and readiness to learn and operate with new technology platforms, tools, whether low code like Celonis or Fabric or pro code like new cloud infrastructure
MSc degree in Machine Learning, Data Science and Artificial Intelligence, Data Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science or similar
Professional-level Finnish and English language proficiency


The Following Is Considered a Plus


Interest in continuously learning and applying new technologies and platforms; generative AI, RAG, AI Agents, among others
Experience with various tech stacks, e.g. AWS Databricks, Azure Data Factory, Microsoft Fabric, DBT, Apache Kafka, Terraform, or similar
Experience in utilising data from business technology platforms, such as ERP, CRM or similar (SAP, Salesforce, ServiceNow, etc.)
Experience in transforming data for process and content intelligence purposes
Previous consulting experience


You don’t need to tick every box! What matters most is your drive to make a real impact!

What We Offer

We are building the best work environment for data, AI and business process professionals. To get there, we invest in the well-being and work-life balance as well as the professional development of our people. We operate on trust and transparency rather than titles, fostering a high-autonomy, growth-oriented environment where collaboration, impact, and everyone’s unique thoughts and ideas are valued.

In addition to the usual perks, such as work tools of your choice and extensive healthcare benefits, we also offer our employees the opportunity to invest in our rapidly growing company via an employee share program.

Work with Leading Nordic Companies

At Vuono Group, you’ll collaborate with Nordic clients on a range of challenging digital projects. Along the way, you’ll have the opportunity to influence technical decisions, architecture, and how solutions are built. Explore some of our client cases here.

Next steps

We’re always on the lookout for new talent, which is why we recruit on a rolling basis. If this opportunity feels like the right fit for you, take the next step and submit your application! Once we receive your application, we’ll be in touch.

Our recruitment process typically includes at least two interviews, giving you the chance to meet some of our team members and giving us the opportunity to get to know you better: as a professional, a colleague, and a person. Reference checks are also a standard part of our process. We look forward to hearing from you!

Vuono Group is a process AI company. We create superior business processes through data and AI. Our growing team of kind and ambitious people is on a mission to turn AI into real, measurable business impact. Take the next step, explore our open positions and connect with us to find your next opportunity!

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Finnair is a diverse community of professionals who work ambitiously, with a big heart and strong commitment. A strong safety culture is our priority and at the core of our everyday work — something we never take for granted. We are also committed to doing our share in solving the climate challenge by determinedly following our science-based pathway to decarbonize our operations.

We find meaning in our purpose: With our heart in Finland, we make every journey better, today and tomorrow. Our values — Always Safe, Customer at Heart and Stronger Together — guide us in everything we do.

Intelligent Operations is a new organization within Finnair Digital, focused on accelerating the digital transformation of airline operations. We bring together expertise in technology, data, architecture, integrations, and operational development to build reliable digital capabilities that support operational workflows and decision-making across the airline.

We are now looking for a

(Full Stack) Data Scientist

to join our Intelligent Operations team at Finnair.

In this role, you will develop data science, AI, machine learning, and optimization solutions that support operational decision-making across Finnair. You will work with complex operational problems and help turn data, models, and analytical methods into practical solutions that improve situational awareness and support real-time, tactical, and day-to-day decision-making.

The role combines strong data science expertise with an interest in software development. Our data science solutions are developed with production use in mind, which means attention to code quality, testing, maintainability, deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement.

You will work closely with operational stakeholders, Business Analysts, Data Engineers, Software Developers, architects, and other Digital teams to understand business needs and develop reliable analytical and AI-driven solutions for an operationally critical environment.

Main Responsibilities


Work with operational stakeholders and Digital teams to understand business needs, identify operational challenges, and translate them into data science, AI, machine learning, or optimization solutions.
Design, develop, validate, and deploy models and analytical solutions that support operational decision-making.
Build scalable, maintainable, and production-ready data science solutions using modern software engineering practices.
Develop clean, tested, and reusable code, applying test-driven development principles where appropriate.
Contribute to MLOps practices, including model deployment, monitoring, observability, and continuous improvement.
Ensure high standards for data quality, model validation, governance, and responsible use of AI.
Communicate findings, model outcomes, risks, and recommendations clearly to both technical and business stakeholders.
Support the continuous improvement of data science, AI, optimization, software development, and MLOps practices within Intelligent Operations.


Our expectations


Master’s degree or higher in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Engineering, Econometrics, Operations Research, or another relevant quantitative field.
Minimum 3–5 years of experience in data science, machine learning, operations research, or a similar analytical role.
Strong Python programming skills, good understanding of statistics, probability, optimization, and machine learning methods, and experience developing analytical, machine learning, or optimization solutions for real-world business problems.
Interest in software development and production-ready data science solutions, with familiarity in practices such as version control, testing, code quality, CI/CD, test-driven development, model deployment, monitoring, and lifecycle management.
Ability to assess data quality, validate analytical outputs, and apply responsible AI principles such as transparency, validation, and governance.
Practical problem-solving mindset and ability to communicate findings, model outcomes, risks, and recommendations clearly to both technical and business stakeholders.
Experience with cloud-based development, preferably AWS, aviation, airline operations, or another operationally critical environment is considered an advantage.
Fluency in English is required. Finnish language skills are considered an advantage.


Working at Finnair

We offer you an exciting opportunity in the multifaceted and fast-changing aviation and travel industry, along with a great team and wide personal development opportunities. At Finnair, ambitious, competent, and motivated people can really make a difference.

We are happy to enable our employees to enrich their lives and fulfill their travel dreams by offering a wide leisure travel benefit. We also support the health and wellbeing of our employees through our own Sickness Fund insurance benefit, Epassi benefits for sport, culture, wellbeing and commuting, gyms around the Finnair campus, and activities organized by our hobby clubs.

Are you interested? Please submit your application no later than 24 July 2026.

Please note that the hiring process includes a pre-employment security clearance and a drug test.

In case you have any questions about the role, please contact Beenish Qaiser, Director, Intelligent Operations, by phone on Wednesdays 1 July or 8 July between 14:00 and 16:00 at +358 45 359 8008.

Please note that we do not process applications submitted by email.

Our people are key to our success and the foundation that makes Finnair unique. We are committed to creating a safe work environment where all our people can thrive and succeed. We cherish diversity and strive to employ people with different backgrounds and experiences, bringing together multiple views to foster our business success. According to our employee survey, respecting diversity is at a high level in our work community.

We offer you an exciting opportunity in the multifaceted and fast-changing aviation and travel industry, along with a great team and wide personal development opportunities. At Finnair, ambitious, competent, and motivated people can really make a difference.

We are happy to enable our employees to enrich their lives and fulfill their travel dreams by offering a wide leisure travel benefit. We also support the health and wellbeing of our employees through our own Sickness Fund insurance benefit, Epassi benefits for sport, culture, wellbeing and commuting, gyms around the Finnair campus, and activities organized by our hobby clubs.

Are you interested? Please submit your application no later than 24 July 2026.

Please note that the hiring process includes a pre-employment security clearance and a drug test.

In case you have any questions about the role, please contact Beenish Qaiser, Director, Intelligent Operations, by phone on Wednesdays 1 July or 8 July between 14:00 and 16:00 at +358 45 359 8008.

Please note that we do not process applications submitted by email.

Our people are key to our success and the foundation that makes Finnair unique. We are committed to creating a safe work environment where all our people can thrive and succeed. We cherish diversity and strive to employ people with different backgrounds and experiences, bringing together multiple views to foster our business success. According to our employee survey, respecting diversity is at a high level in our work community.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
What you’ll do


Train, test, and ship models that power Peec AI’s recommendations - helping customers boost their visibility in AI search
Develop algorithms that extract actionable insights from AI search behavior, creating data-driven recommendations that help brands increase their AI Search visibility
Own the full model lifecycle from experimentation to production deployment, working closely with engineering to integrate ML solutions into our systems
Design and implement data pipelines to ingest, process, and analyze large volumes of data


What we’re looking for


Proven backend development skills in Python with experience building APIs, data pipelines, or ML infrastructure, and familiarity with tools like FastAPI, Docker, and cloud platforms (preferably GCP)
Deep curiosity about how LLMs work, with the ability to reverse-engineer AI search behavior and translate patterns into actionable product features
Track record of taking projects from research to production in a fast-moving startup environment, with strong problem-solving skills and comfort working with ambiguous, evolving problems
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to customers and stakeholders


Our Data Science Stack


Languages: Python, SQL
Libraries: Pandas, NumPy, HuggingFace, PyTorch, TensorFlow, ONNX
Backend: GCP, Cloud Functions, Firestore, Postgres, AlloyDB, BigQuery
AI Models: OpenAI, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini, Llama, etc.


Bonus Points


Contributions to open-source projects
Deployed side/hobby projects that we can check out
Presented research papers at top ML or AI conferences
Having started a company before or worked at a high-growth startup
Fluency in Typescript


What we offer


Exciting and challenging work with real impact and ownership at one of Europe’s fastest-growing Series A startups
Regular team events and off-sites
Aggressive equity compensation package
Paid Dinner & Uber home when working late
The most beautiful office space and work environment in Berlin

Title: Mid/Senior Data Scientist (Incogni)
HTML:
Our Data Science team, working with Surfshark and Incogni products, is looking for a skilled Data Scientist. Our projects cover a broad spectrum of data science challenges, from time series modeling to NLP and generative AI We follow a value-driven approach and are deeply committed to contributing to the growth and success of our products. If you are enthusiastic about cybersecurity, data protection, and data science, we look forward to your application!

If you want to:

  • Design, build, and productionize NLP-heavy machine learning systems, including text classification, entity extraction, semantic similarity, and LLM-based workflows;
  • Write clean, efficient, and testable Python code for data pipelines, feature engineering, modeling, and evaluation.
  • Own models end to end: problem framing, data exploration, model selection, validation, deployment, and iteration.
  • Work closely with product and engineering teams to turn ambiguous problems into concrete, data-driven solutions.
  • Evaluate and apply modern NLP approaches, from classical methods to transformer-based and generative models, with a pragmatic focus on impact.
  • Communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, focusing on decisions, trade-offs, and outcomes rather than theory.

And you can check off:

  • Proven experience in applied data science or machine learning, with a track record of owning and delivering models to production.
  • Strong NLP background, including hands-on experience with text modeling, embeddings, transformers, and LLM-based systems.
  • Advanced Python skills, with the ability to write production-quality code.
  • Solid SQL skills and experience working with large, imperfect, real-world datasets.
  • Strong understanding of machine learning fundamentals, model evaluation, and common failure modes.
  • Ability to translate business problems into modeling approaches and explain results clearly without relying on jargon.
  • Experience working in modern engineering environments, including code reviews, testing, version control, and CI/CD.

Here’s the deal:

  • Growth and learning opportunities: time dedicated to learning, conferences, online learning platforms, and books for your professional development;
  • Health and wellness: we want you to feel and be your best. That's why we offer various benefits, from online workouts and a physical coach to health insurance and regular mental health checks;
  • Tools of your choice: choose the technical equipment and tools you need to do your best work;
  • Community and celebrations: get ready for long-lasting traditions such as yearly workation, Friday get-togethers, various team buildings and company celebrations;
  • Work-life balance: We encourage working in the office, with the possibility of up to two remote days per week. Plus, with our WFA policy, you can work from almost anywhere in the world;
  • More days off: additional vacation days depending on your tenure;
  • Premium Surfshark accounts: for you, your family, and friends;
  • Gross salary: 3500-6800 Eur/month. Although it may vary depending on your skills and experience.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Csatlakozz a Quadron csapatához Data Scientist pozícióban!




Érdekel, hogyan lehet az adatból valódi üzleti és biztonsági értéket teremteni?

Otthonosan mozogsz az adattudomány, a gépi tanulás és a komplex analitikai megoldások világában?




A Quadron Analitika és Kiberbiztonság Zrt.-nél az adatok nem csak számok: olyan intelligens megoldások alapjai, amelyek egy biztonságosabb digitális jövőt építenek. Most téged keresünk, hogy együtt alakítsuk a fejlett analitika következő szintjét.




Mivel foglalkoznál nálunk?

Komplex adatelemzési keretrendszerek és analitikai modellek tervezése és fejlesztése
Adatintegrációs folyamatok megalkotása és implementálása
Szöveganalitikai modellek fejlesztése
Kép- és videóelemzési megoldások készítése
Adatmenedzsment, adatkonszolidáció és adattisztítás
Hálózatelemzés
Adatvizualizáció, hogy az eredmények valóban érthetőek és használhatóak legyenek




Téged keresünk, ha rendelkezel az alábbiakkal:

Legalább 3 év releváns tapasztalat adatmérnök vagy adattudós szerepkörben
Magabiztos Python és SQL tudás
Tapasztalat adatmodellezésben és adatintegrációban
Jártasság statisztikai elemzésben, adatmodellezésben és gépi tanulásban
Fejlett analitikus gondolkodásmód és problémamegoldó készség

SAS ismeretek
Befejezett felsőfokú tanulmányok matematikai, statisztikai, mérnöki, informatikai vagy gazdasági területen
Kiváló kommunikációs készség




Miért érdemes a Quadronhoz csatlakozni?

Izgalmas, magas hozzáadott értékű projekteken dolgozhatsz
Támogató, befogadó csapat, ahol számítanak az ötleteid
Éves bónusz
Extra szabadnap a születésnapodon, valamint véradás esetén
Utazási támogatás

Title: Data Scientist (all genders)
Description:
The group you’ll be a part of

In the Global Products Group, we are dedicated to excellence in the design and engineering of Lam's etch and deposition products. We drive innovation to ensure our cutting-edge solutions are helping to solve the biggest challenges in the semiconductor industry.

The impact you’ll make

You will turn complex data into clear, actionable insights to support data-driven decisions. By building scalable solutions and working with cross-functional teams, you will improve analysis and reporting while ensuring data quality and driving innovation. This will help to advance equipment intelligence across the company.

What You’ll Do


Build production-grade Python packages.
Follow and build project scaffolds (PowerShell), follow coding standards, and reproducible workflows.
Transform analyses into standardized currently best-known methods (cBKM)
Process data and automate data flows; manage and optimize data structure & data bases for big data sets.
Interact with product and service teams to identify questions and issues for data analysis and experiments.
Interpret and communicate data analysis results and experiments to product, service & business managers.
Design, build and maintain dashboards.
Process confidential data and information according to guidelines.
Support to setup iterating reports, analysis and dashboards.


Who We’re Looking For


Bachelor or Master Degree: Statistics/Computer Science/Data Science/ /Industrial Engineering
Expert knowledge of Python, statistical modelling (frequentist and Bayesian) and data visualization
Intermediate knowledge of PowerShell, R and SQL
AI expert knowledge
Project management
Comfortable with non-recurrent, high-complexity engineering analyses that drive product decisions
Experience with Industry 4.0 (EI): Best-case has worked in an actual company having Industry 4.0 stuff running
Made predictive maintenance models, deployed and managed them
Expert knowledge of Docker
Skill in project management i.e. being able to break down complex visions into actual work packages
Structured problem solving mindset


What We're Offering

This position is subject to the Austrian Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) for Employees in the Metal Technology Industry in the occupation group F. A higher paymentis negotiable depending on education, expertise and skills. The monthly salary is paid 14 times per year.

The position is hired through a third party agency.

Villach - a great place to live and work

Follow the link www.welcome2villach.at and you will find on the spot the most important information aboutliving, working and studying in and around Villach/Austria.

Our commitment

We believe it is important for every person to feel valued, included, and empowered to achieve their full potential. By bringing unique individuals and viewpoints together, we achieve extraordinary results.

Lam Research ("Lam" or the "Company") is an equal opportunity employer. Lam is committed to and reaffirms support of equal opportunity in employment and non-discrimination in employment policies, practices and procedures on the basis of race, religious creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions), gender, gender identity, gender expression, age, sexual orientation, or military and veteran status or any other category protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws. It is the Company's intention to comply with all applicable laws and regulations. Company policy prohibits unlawful discrimination against applicants or employees.

Lam offers a variety of work location models based on the needs of each role. Our hybrid roles combine the benefits of on-site collaboration with colleagues and the flexibility to work remotely and fall into two categories – On-site Flex and Virtual Flex. ‘On-site Flex’ you’ll work 3+ days per week on-site at a Lam or customer/supplier location, with the opportunity to work remotely for the balance of the week. ‘Virtual Flex’ you’ll work 1-2 days per week on-site at a Lam or customer/supplier location, and remotely the rest of the time.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Company Description

Our Mission Statement

Digital and human resources at the center of the sustainable development of our society.

In a world of continuous transformation, accelerated by technological developments and societal challenges, it is necessary to adapt in an ongoing, agile way to meet the challenges of the future.

About Inetum

Inetum is a European leader in digital services. For businesses, public sector organizations, and society as a whole, the group’s 26,000 consultants and specialists work every day to create tangible digital impact: solutions that contribute to performance, innovation, and the common good.

With a presence in 19 countries, working closely with local communities, and alongside its major software developer partners, Inetum supports organizations in their digital transformation challenges with proximity, flexibility, and responsibility. Driven by its purpose, Inetum champions a vision of technology that is useful and well-managed, capable of unlocking the full potential of organizations and society: “Let’s make tech right.”

In 2025, the group generated revenue of 2.2 billion euros.

More information at: www.inetum.com

Job Description


Extract, prepare and analyze complex datasets
Build and implement machine learning models to solve business problems
Develop data visualizations and dashboards to communicate findings
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business requirements and translate them into data solutions
Clean, preprocess, and organize raw data for analysis
Conduct A/B testing and evaluate model performance.


Qualifications

Profile:


Strong programming skills in Python
Experience in building and training ML
Experience with SQL
Experience with JupyterHub, MLFlow, Airflow and Dask is a plus
Experience with Elastic is a plus
Experience in managing/processing large data sets
Strong communication skills to convey complex insights to non-technical audiences
Knowledge of Agile/Scrum methodologies.


Benefits

Additional Information


Full access to foreign language learning platform
Personalized access to tech learning platforms
Tailored workshops and trainings to sustain your growth
Medical insurance
Meal tickets
Monthly budget to allocate on flexible benefit platform
Access to 7 Card services
Wellbeing activities and gatherings

Title: Data Scientist – TV-Insight
Description:
Are you passionate about creating innovative data-driven products that reshape industries?

TV-Insight is an exciting digital spin-off within Red Bull, located in Elsbethen (Austria), dedicated to revolutionizing the TV measurement and advertisement industry. As a Data Scientist, you will be at the forefront of creating and improving the analytical foundation of our novel product, capable of handling multiple millions of classification tasks every second. Working alongside a team of talented data scientists, data engineers, analysts, and business stakeholders, you will play a key role in defining the success of a state-of-the-art analytics product.

All the responsibilities we'll trust you with:

Model Ownership & Production Deployment

You take ownership of the development and deployment of machine learning models for a dedicated TV-Insight market. This includes building and improving real-time machine learning applications as well as developing model components that feed into real-time projection processing. Working closely with a highly specialized team of engineers and data analysts, you ensure a strong understanding of the technical implementation while setting the highest standards for statistical modelling, creating direct business impact from day one.

Exploratory Analysis, Model Understanding & Communication

You conduct thorough exploratory data analyses to evaluate input data and gain a deep understanding of model behaviour and performance. In both research tasks and day-to-day work, you uphold best practices in modelling approaches and production-quality code. You communicate your insights in a clear and accessible way through dashboards and reports, enabling data-driven decision-making for management and key stakeholders.

Continuous Innovation & Technical Excellence

You continuously stay up to date with technological and industry developments to expand your skill set and strengthen your ability to innovate. You critically assess the potential of new methods, tools, and trends, and proactively implement your own ideas in small-scale experiments to test and validate their expected benefits before scaling them into production.

Your areas of knowledge and expertise that matter most for this role:


Master’s degree or PhD in a highly quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related discipline
3+ years of professional experience in quantitative analytics, data science, or applied statistics.
Strong hands-on experience in statistical modelling using R or Python, with solid experience working with databases and query languages such as SQL.
Proven expertise in applying core statistical methods, including regression, classification, and hypothesis testing, to real-world business problems.
Ability to translate complex analytical results into clear, compelling visualizations and dashboards using tools such as ggplot, R Shiny, or Data Studio.
Strong presentation and communication skills, with the ability to explain complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences and collaborate effectively in cross-functional teams.


Due to legal reasons, we are obliged to disclose the minimum salary according to the collective agreement for this position, which is € 2.362 gross per month. However, our attractive compensation package is based on market-oriented salaries and is therefore significantly above the stated minimum salary.

As an employer, we value diversity and support people in developing their potential and strengths, realizing their ideas and seizing opportunities. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We welcome applications from all members of society irrespective of age, skin colour, religion, gender, sexual orientation or origin.
